title: AttestListReport
description: Envelope emitted by `aegis-boot attest list --json`. Scans the host's attestation directory, attempts to parse each file, and reports either a parsed summary or a parse-error placeholder per entry. Enables monitoring / fleet tools to audit chain-of-custody across all flashed sticks on a host.

attestations.description: One entry per file found. See [`AttestListEntry`] for the success/error shape selection.

attestations_dir.description: Host filesystem path of the attestations directory scanned (typically `$XDG_DATA_HOME/aegis-boot/attestations/`).

count.description: Number of files scanned (including parse-failure entries).

tool_version.description: `aegis-boot` binary version that produced this envelope.

AttestListEntry.description:
One entry in an [`AttestListReport`]. Two mutually-exclusive wire shapes via serde's `untagged` tagging:

* **Success** — a successful parse, reporting the manifest's headline fields. The `error` field is absent. * **Error** — the file existed but could not be parsed. Only `manifest_path` + `error` are emitted; the summary fields are absent.

Schemars emits this as a JSON Schema `oneOf` so consumers know to branch on the presence of the `error` field.

anyOf.description: Placeholder for a file that existed but failed to parse.

AttestListError.description: Parse-failure entry inside an [`AttestListReport`]. Consumer decision: show the operator which file failed + why, so they can audit / repair / delete.

error.description: Human-readable error message from the parser.

manifest_path.description: Host filesystem path of the unparseable file.

AttestListSuccess.description: Successfully-parsed attestation summary inside an [`AttestListReport`]. Deliberately a strict subset of the full [`Attestation`] — enough to drive a dashboard without requiring consumers to re-parse each file. Full detail is one `aegis-boot attest show <path>` away.

disk_guid.description: GPT disk GUID from the attestation's target info.

iso_count.description: Number of [`IsoRecord`] entries inside the attestation.

manifest_path.description: Host filesystem path of the attestation manifest file.

operator.description: Operator that ran the flash (from the attestation).

schema_version.description: Schema version declared inside the attestation manifest (see [`ATTESTATION_SCHEMA_VERSION`]).
